caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: 4-digit 5419 is materially contaminated by veterinary services and is not a usable representation of 541910 staffing.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The high residual share is a modeling consequence of the bad proxy, not an observed occupation distribution.
inputs.pi_dist: Sources support AI/data competitive change, not a measured 541910 pass-through rate.
inputs.pi_moat: No source in this packet measures lower-middle-market client retention or founder dependency.
inputs.t50_years: No public full-industry effective-adoption percentage was reopened.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Not an observed NAICS adoption statistic; it intentionally remains below 50%, requiring positive t50.
inputs.historical_analogs: No causal historical study is claimed.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Unobserved estimate; not a demographic claim.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of evidence here is not evidence of absence.
inputs.active_consolidators: These buyers and targets are global/data/technology adjacent and are not direct evidence of small US 541910 acquisition appetite.
inputs.buy_mult: No disclosed lower-middle-market 541910 transaction multiple was reopened; the cited marketing-agency category is a proxy.
inputs.exit_mult: Strategic announcements disclose no transaction multiples and are materially larger/adjacent to the intended target set.
inputs.pricing_structure: No reopened source quantifies the recurring-revenue share for this NAICS code.

Validator: The official Census 541910 URL resolved, but its code-specific definition was not exposed in the reopened text rendering, so direct page-level verification of that scope claim was incomplete.

Validator: Source S2 resolves to the 2024 GRIT Business & Innovation Report, not the stated 2025 report; its public page supports GenAI replacing work processes but not all of the packet's exact qualitative characterizations.
Validator: Kantar's Numerator acquisition is from July 2021, so it shows historical buyer participation rather than current 2025-2026 consolidation cadence.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $318,207 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541910); class <5: 2,509 firms, avg revenue/firm $530,196 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $82,976 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 435 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,835,349 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $287,232 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 296 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,275,365 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $512,595 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 309 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,129,165 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,585,214 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 120 firms, avg revenue/firm $46,203,783 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,230,892 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 78 firms, avg revenue/firm $191,354,487 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $29,946,977 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 429

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: The supplied proxy is dominated by veterinary occupations, so the listed non-research roles are assigned cautiously and the residual is the transparent approximation for actual research, operations and qualitative labor. AI accelerates analysis and reporting, not human sampling or senior counsel.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541910 ($8,069,771,700) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541910 ($26,698,162,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

Validator: The official Census 541910 URL resolved, but its code-specific definition was not exposed in the reopened text rendering, so direct page-level verification of that scope claim was incomplete.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, exact artifact identity, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. All eight registered sources were reopened. The low-confidence kill thesis remains directionally useful and avoids terminal/capture double counting, but publication must retain 13 authored caveats plus three validator caveats for incomplete Census page rendering, the GRIT year/content mismatch and the age of the Kantar acquisition evidence. No finding is shown to reverse a factor or materially change the verdict.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 541910
sources.what_it_supports: Official definition: establishments systematically gather, record, tabulate and present marketing and public-opinion data.

sources.title: 2025 GRIT Business & Innovation Report
sources.what_it_supports: Greenbook describes AI adoption as a permanent market shift while emphasizing human-centered insights and a trust problem around good-enough insights.

sources.title: ICC/ESOMAR International Code on Market, Opinion and Social Research and Data Analytics, 2025
sources.what_it_supports: The code defines primary data and distinguishes a human individual from a synthetic, virtual or digitally created persona.

sources.title: Kantar acquisition of Numerator completes
sources.what_it_supports: Kantar completed acquisition of Numerator, a tech-driven consumer and market-intelligence company; financial terms were not released.

sources.title: NIQ signs agreement to acquire Gastrograph AI
sources.what_it_supports: NIQ announced acquisition of a sensory-insights AI platform to provide faster, lower-cost product innovation capabilities.

sources.title: NIQ acquires Mtrix
sources.what_it_supports: NIQ states the Mtrix acquisition was its latest of nine tuck-ins enhancing analytics and retailer tools.

sources.title: WPP acquires InfoSum
sources.what_it_supports: WPP acquired InfoSum to provide AI-enabled marketing intelligence and privacy-safe data collaboration.

sources.title: Windsor Drake EBITDA Multiples by Industry, 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Its Q1 2026 observations show marketing/digital-agency EBITDA ranges of 3.0x–4.5x for $1m–$3m and 4.0x–6.5x for $3m–$10m, with retention and specialization as drivers.

title: Marketing Research and Public Opinion Polling
